forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+---
+assignees: []
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: c180
+title: 'browser tests: drill-in setFocus assertions check stale ui.setFocus dispatch contract'
+---
+Three browser-mode test files assert that drill-in / Enter / Escape / ArrowDown flows fan out to `dispatch_command("ui.setFocus", { args: { scope_chain: [...] } })`. They fail on pristine HEAD because the production code path in `entity-focus-context.tsx::setFocus` (lines 380-396) calls `spatial.focus(fq)` (which invokes `spatial_focus`) when a `SpatialFocusProvider` is present and never reaches the test-harness fallback that dispatches `ui.setFocus` (lines 399-405). All three failing test files do wrap in `SpatialFocusProvider`, so they exercise the production path.
+
+Failing tests (5 across 3 files):
+- kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.enter-drill-in.browser.test.tsx
+  * `enter_on_focused_column_drills_into_first_card` (line 639)
+  * `enter_on_focused_column_with_remembered_focus_drills_into_remembered_card` (line 710)
+- kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.field-enter-drill.browser.test.tsx
+  * `enter_on_pill_field_drills_into_first_pill` (line 533)
+  * `escape_from_pill_drills_back_to_field_zone` (line 690)
+- kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector.kernel-focus-advance.browser.test.tsx
+  * `ArrowDown from the last field stays put, kernel's focused key remains the last field` (line 518)
+
+Symptom: `dispatchArgs?.scope_chain?.[0]` is `undefined` because no `dispatch_command(ui.setFocus, ...)` call was made â€” the test stubs `spatial_drill_in` to return a moniker, and the React closure forwards it via `setFocusRef.current(result)`, which calls `spatial_focus(fq)`, not `ui.setFocus`.
+
+Fix direction: rewrite each assertion to pin the production contract:
+  expect a `spatial_focus` invocation with the resolved `fq` mapped from the kernel-returned moniker, and rely on the simulated `focus-changed` event echo (which the in-test `defaultInvokeImpl` already emits via queueMicrotask) to drive subsequent React state assertions.
+
+These failures are pre-existing on branch `kanban` at commit 35a106634 â€” they are not caused by the `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/registry.rs` overlap-warning change.
+
+What I tried:
+- Ran full workspace tests + clippy â€” all Rust passes clean.
+- Reproduced the 5 failures both with the user's working-tree changes and after stashing them. Pristine HEAD also fails. So the changes under review did not introduce these failures.
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value: browser tests: drill-in setFocus assertions check stale ui.setFocus dispatch contract

Three browser-mode test files assert that drill-in / Enter / Escape / ArrowDown flows fan out to `dispatch_command("ui.setFocus", { args: { scope_chain: [...] } })`. They fail on pristine HEAD because the production code path in `entity-focus-context.tsx::setFocus` (lines 380-396) calls `spatial.focus(fq)` (which invokes `spatial_focus`) when a `SpatialFocusProvider` is present and never reaches the test-harness fallback that dispatches `ui.setFocus` (lines 399-405). All three failing test files do wrap in `SpatialFocusProvider`, so they exercise the production path.

Failing tests (5 across 3 files):
- kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.enter-drill-in.browser.test.tsx
  * `enter_on_focused_column_drills_into_first_card` (line 639)
  * `enter_on_focused_column_with_remembered_focus_drills_into_remembered_card` (line 710)
- kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.field-enter-drill.browser.test.tsx
  * `enter_on_pill_field_drills_into_first_pill` (line 533)
  * `escape_from_pill_drills_back_to_field_zone` (line 690)
- kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector.kernel-focus-advance.browser.test.tsx
  * `ArrowDown from the last field stays put, kernel's focused key remains the last field` (line 518)

Symptom: `dispatchArgs?.scope_chain?.[0]` is `undefined` because no `dispatch_command(ui.setFocus, ...)` call was made â€” the test stubs `spatial_drill_in` to return a moniker, and the React closure forwards it via `setFocusRef.current(result)`, which calls `spatial_focus(fq)`, not `ui.setFocus`.

Fix direction: rewrite each assertion to pin the production contract:
  expect a `spatial_focus` invocation with the resolved `fq` mapped from the kernel-returned moniker, and rely on the simulated `focus-changed` event echo (which the in-test `defaultInvokeImpl` already emits via queueMicrotask) to drive subsequent React state assertions.

These failures are pre-existing on branch `kanban` at commit 35a106634 â€” they are not caused by the `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/registry.rs` overlap-warning change.

What I tried:
- Ran full workspace tests + clippy â€” all Rust passes clean.
- Reproduced the 5 failures both with the user's working-tree changes and after stashing them. Pristine HEAD also fails. So the changes under review did not introduce these failures.

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -2,27 +2,23 @@
 assignees: []
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: c180
-title: 'browser tests: drill-in setFocus assertions check stale ui.setFocus dispatch contract'
+title: 'RESOLVED: browser tests stale ui.setFocus contract â€” fixed inline during /test run'
 ---
-Three browser-mode test files assert that drill-in / Enter / Escape / ArrowDown flows fan out to `dispatch_command("ui.setFocus", { args: { scope_chain: [...] } })`. They fail on pristine HEAD because the production code path in `entity-focus-context.tsx::setFocus` (lines 380-396) calls `spatial.focus(fq)` (which invokes `spatial_focus`) when a `SpatialFocusProvider` is present and never reaches the test-harness fallback that dispatches `ui.setFocus` (lines 399-405). All three failing test files do wrap in `SpatialFocusProvider`, so they exercise the production path.
+RESOLVED â€” fixed inline during a /test run on branch `kanban`.

-Failing tests (5 across 3 files):
-- kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.enter-drill-in.browser.test.tsx
-  * `enter_on_focused_column_drills_into_first_card` (line 639)
-  * `enter_on_focused_column_with_remembered_focus_drills_into_remembered_card` (line 710)
-- kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.field-enter-drill.browser.test.tsx
-  * `enter_on_pill_field_drills_into_first_pill` (line 533)
-  * `escape_from_pill_drills_back_to_field_zone` (line 690)
-- kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector.kernel-focus-advance.browser.test.tsx
-  * `ArrowDown from the last field stays put, kernel's focused key remains the last field` (line 518)
+Three browser-mode test files asserted that drill-in / Enter / Escape / ArrowDown flows fan out to `dispatch_command("ui.setFocus", { args: { scope_chain: [...] } })` with the kernel-returned moniker at chain[0]. The assertions were stale relative to the production kernel-bridge architecture documented on `EntityFocusProvider`:

-Symptom: `dispatchArgs?.scope_chain?.[0]` is `undefined` because no `dispatch_command(ui.setFocus, ...)` call was made â€” the test stubs `spatial_drill_in` to return a moniker, and the React closure forwards it via `setFocusRef.current(result)`, which calls `spatial_focus(fq)`, not `ui.setFocus`.
+  - Production `FocusActions.setFocus(fq)` routes through `spatial.focus(fq)` â†’ `spatial_focus` IPC, NOT through a `dispatch_command(ui.setFocus, ...)`.
+  - The `subscribeFocusChanged` bridge inside `EntityFocusProvider` does dispatch `ui.setFocus` after each `focus-changed` event, but its `scope_chain` is built by looking up `payload.next_fq` (an FQM) in the entity-scope registry. The simulator's `spatial_focus` handler emits the raw moniker as `next_fq`, which doesn't match any FQM-keyed registry entry â€” so the chain was empty, never `[<moniker>, ...]`.

-Fix direction: rewrite each assertion to pin the production contract:
-  expect a `spatial_focus` invocation with the resolved `fq` mapped from the kernel-returned moniker, and rely on the simulated `focus-changed` event echo (which the in-test `defaultInvokeImpl` already emits via queueMicrotask) to drive subsequent React state assertions.
+Fixes (production-aligned):
+- kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.enter-drill-in.browser.test.tsx â€” replaced the `setFocusDispatch` lookup at lines 628-639 and 699-710 with a direct `spatial_focus` invocation assertion (`focusCall.fq === "task:t1"` / `"task:t2"`). This pins the same end-to-end contract â€” drill-in resolves through the kernel â€” without depending on bridge-derived scope chains.
+- kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.field-enter-drill.browser.test.tsx â€” replaced the `setFocusDispatches()` helper with `spatialFocusCalls()` (and updated both call sites' assertions and rationale comments).
+- kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector.kernel-focus-advance.browser.test.tsx â€” the `ArrowDown from the last field stays put` test was inverted: real BeamNavStrategy at bodyâ†’down does iter 0 (no peer) â†’ iter 1 (no parent-zone peer) â†’ drill-out fallback returns the parent panel `task:T1`. Renamed the test, rewrote the assertion to the correct drill-out behavior, and added a `waitFor(titleField.fq)` gate before the navigation loop so the test no longer races first-field auto-focus.

-These failures are pre-existing on branch `kanban` at commit 35a106634 â€” they are not caused by the `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/registry.rs` overlap-warning change.
+Also removed an unused `registerScopeArgs` helper from `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.spatial.test.tsx` (TypeScript noUnused checks were failing under `tsc --noEmit`).

-What I tried:
-- Ran full workspace tests + clippy â€” all Rust passes clean.
-- Reproduced the 5 failures both with the user's working-tree changes and after stashing them. Pristine HEAD also fails. So the changes under review did not introduce these failures. #test-failure
\ No newline at end of file
+Verified clean:
+- `cargo test --workspace` (now `cargo nextest run --workspace`) â€” 13576 passed, 0 failed.
+- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` â€” clean.
+- `npm test` (kanban-app/ui) â€” 1885 passed, 0 failed (4 pre-existing intentional skips remain â€” see follow-up tasks).
\ No newline at end of file

The assertions were stale relative to the production kernel-bridge architecture documented on `EntityFocusProvider`:
+Failing tests (5 across 3 files):
+- kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.enter-drill-in.browser.test.tsx
+  * `enter_on_focused_column_drills_into_first_card` (line 639)
+  * `enter_on_focused_column_with_remembered_focus_drills_into_remembered_card` (line 710)
+- kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.field-enter-drill.browser.test.tsx
+  * `enter_on_pill_field_drills_into_first_pill` (line 533)
+  * `escape_from_pill_drills_back_to_field_zone` (line 690)
+- kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector.kernel-focus-advance.browser.test.tsx
+  * `ArrowDown from the last field stays put, kernel's focused key remains the last field` (line 518)

-  - Production `FocusActions.setFocus(fq)` routes through `spatial.focus(fq)` â†’ `spatial_focus` IPC, NOT through a `dispatch_command(ui.setFocus, ...)`.
-  - The `subscribeFocusChanged` bridge inside `EntityFocusProvider` does dispatch `ui.setFocus` after each `focus-changed` event, but its `scope_chain` is built by looking up `payload.next_fq` (an FQM) in the entity-scope registry. The simulator's `spatial_focus` handler emits the raw moniker as `next_fq`, which doesn't match any FQM-keyed registry entry â€” so the chain was empty, never `[<moniker>, ...]`.
+Symptom: `dispatchArgs?.scope_chain?.[0]` is `undefined` because no `dispatch_command(ui.setFocus, ...)` call was made â€” the test stubs `spatial_drill_in` to return a moniker, and the React closure forwards it via `setFocusRef.current(result)`, which calls `spatial_focus(fq)`, not `ui.setFocus`.

-Fixes (production-aligned):
-- kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.enter-drill-in.browser.test.tsx â€” replaced the `setFocusDispatch` lookup at lines 628-639 and 699-710 with a direct `spatial_focus` invocation assertion (`focusCall.fq === "task:t1"` / `"task:t2"`). This pins the same end-to-end contract â€” drill-in resolves through the kernel â€” without depending on bridge-derived scope chains.
-- kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.field-enter-drill.browser.test.tsx â€” replaced the `setFocusDispatches()` helper with `spatialFocusCalls()` (and updated both call sites' assertions and rationale comments).
-- kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector.kernel-focus-advance.browser.test.tsx â€” the `ArrowDown from the last field stays put` test was inverted: real BeamNavStrategy at bodyâ†’down does iter 0 (no peer) â†’ iter 1 (no parent-zone peer) â†’ drill-out fallback returns the parent panel `task:T1`. Renamed the test, rewrote the assertion to the correct drill-out behavior, and added a `waitFor(titleField.fq)` gate before the navigation loop so the test no longer races first-field auto-focus.
+Fix direction: rewrite each assertion to pin the production contract:
+  expect a `spatial_focus` invocation with the resolved `fq` mapped from the kernel-returned moniker, and rely on the simulated `focus-changed` event echo (which the in-test `defaultInvokeImpl` already emits via queueMicrotask) to drive subsequent React state assertions.

-Also removed an unused `registerScopeArgs` helper from `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.spatial.test.tsx` (TypeScript noUnused checks were failing under `tsc --noEmit`).
+These failures are pre-existing on branch `kanban` at commit 35a106634 â€” they are not caused by the `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/registry.rs` overlap-warning change.

-Verified clean:
-- `cargo test --workspace` (now `cargo nextest run --workspace`) â€” 13576 passed, 0 failed.
-- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` â€” clean.
-- `npm test` (kanban-app/ui) â€” 1885 passed, 0 failed (4 pre-existing intentional skips remain â€” see follow-up tasks).
\ No newline at end of file

+- Ran full workspace tests + clippy â€” all Rust passes clean.
+- Reproduced the 5 failures both with the user's working-tree changes and after stashing them. Pristine HEAD also fails. So the changes under review did not introduce these failures. #test-failure
